title: Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple
tagline: Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple, Kerala

overview.short: Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Murugan, located approximately 3.6 kilometres from Chathannoor in Kollam district, Kerala, India.
overview.description: Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Murugan, located approximately 3.6 kilometres from Chathannoor in Kollam district, Kerala, India. Here's a simple historical and mythological story about Kollam, Kerala, India, focusing on fishermen: The Legend of Kollam's Fishermen In ancient Kollam, Lord Vishnu's sixth avatar, Parasurama, created Kerala's coastline. He blessed the fishermen of Kollam with bountiful seas. A humble fisherman, Kumaran, lived in Kollam.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Nadakkal
topPlaces.description: Nadakkal is a small village, 2 km from Kalluvathukkal in Kollam, Kerala, India. It is near National Highway 47.

topPlaces.name: Kalluvathukkal
topPlaces.description: Kalluvathukkal is a village in the Kollam district of the Indian state of Kerala. NH 47 passes through the village.

topPlaces.name: Chathannoor
topPlaces.description: Chathannoor is a town in the Indian state of Kerala situated at the Kollam District on the banks of Ithikkara River. It is about 16 km (9.9 mi) from Kollam(Quilon) City.

topPlaces.name: St. George's Church, Chathannoor
topPlaces.description: St George Orthodox Church, Chathannoor, known locally as Chathannoor Valiya Palli, is a parish of the Thiruvananthapuram (Trivandrum) Diocese of the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church. "Chathannoor Marthamariyam Suriyani Pally," as it was called in the ancient days, was built around AD 1755.

topPlaces.name: Chirakkara
topPlaces.description: Chirakkara is a village in Kollam district in the state of Kerala, India. Chirakkara is 16 km away from the town of Kollam and 8 km away from Paravur Town, which is famous for festivals, estuary, backwaters and beaches.

topPlaces.name: Chathannoor Sree Bhoothanatha Temple
topPlaces.description: Sree Bhoothanatha Temple & Meenadu Sastha temple is one of the well-known temples in Chathannoor neighbourhood of Kollam in Kerala in the peninsular India. One of the largest of its type in Kollam, the Nedum Kuthira of Chathannoor Sree Bhoothanatha Temple and Meenadu Sastha temple is held during the festival.

topPlaces.name: Velinalloor
topPlaces.description: Velinalloor is a village in Kollam district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Kanjiramvila Bhagavathi Temple
topPlaces.description: Kanjiramvila Bhagavathi Temple is a Hindu pilgrim place and also a tourist centre situated in Chathannoor, Kollam, in the Kerala State of India. Many foreigners visit this temple on the occasion of Gajamela.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (46 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (47 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Varinjam Sree Subramanya Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nadakkal, Kalluvathukkal, Chathannoor, St. George's Church, Chathannoor, Chirakkara.
